steps.title: Calculate the annual rate of population change
steps.content:
Using the two known data points: (1996, 61 thousand) and (2016, 225 thousand).

Time elapsed: 2016 − 1996 = 20 years
Population change: 225 − 61 = 164 thousand

Annual rate of change: $$m = \frac{164}{20} = 8.2 \text{ thousand per year}$$

steps.title: Extend the linear model to 2020
steps.content:
From 2016 to 2020 is 4 years. Using the rate of 8.2 thousand per year: $$\text{Population increase} = 8.2 \times 4 = 32.8 \text{ thousand}$$

The 2020 population is: $$x = 225 + 32.8 = 257.8$$

Rounding to the nearest whole number: $$x = 258$$
